name: Pardes Institute of Jewish Studies - Year/Semester Program
durationText: Elul (3-4 weeks), 1 semester, or full year
goodFor: For university graduates who want pluralistic, egalitarian Torah study in seminar-style classes and chavruta pairs, with the flexibility to study for just Elul, a semester, or the full year.
note: Cohort described as diverse/international but not stated as living alongside Israeli students day-to-day — left blank rather than guessed.

name: Conservative Yeshiva
goodFor: For adults of any background wanting a full-time egalitarian, Conservative/Masorti-inflected yeshiva experience, from a single summer or winter session up to a full year.

name: Hevruta Gap-Year Program
cost: $33,000 (includes housing, meals, all programming, trips, staff support and health insurance); limited need-based financial aid available
goodFor: For recent high school graduates who want a Jerusalem gap year split evenly between North American and Israeli peers, pairing Hartman Institute-style pluralistic text study with leadership training.

name: Arevim - Masa Year of Service
goodFor: For young adults (18-21) who want to choose their own social-service focus — health, education, or welfare — alongside political and current-events education, while living independently in Tel Aviv.

name: FZY Year Course
location: Jerusalem and Bat Yam (Tel Aviv area)
goodFor: For UK Jewish young adults wanting a two-city gap year — a Jerusalem semester then a Tel Aviv-area semester — with a free choice of Special Interest Month: kibbutz, yeshiva, hiking, business internship, or surfing.
note: Structured as two distinct city-based semesters rather than a touring circuit or one fixed campus — tagged multi-city-touring as the closer fit, worth a human sanity-check.

name: Habonim Dror UK - Shnat
location: Kibbutz (Shorashim phase) and an Israeli city (Anafim phase)
goodFor: For UK school leavers wanting a Socialist-Zionist, collective-living gap year — three months on kibbutz, then four months living communally while volunteering and teaching English in an Israeli city, plus a Poland trip.
note: Same two-phase (kibbutz + city) structure as FZY — tagged multi-city-touring rather than single-location; worth a human sanity-check on that call.

name: Noam - Drachim Gap Year
organization: Noam (Masorti Judaism's Zionist youth movement) / Masorti Olami
location: Kibbutz Hannaton Education Center, Galilee (and other sites)
goodFor: For young Masorti/Conservative-movement adults from Noam's global network wanting a Galilee-based gap year built around Zionism, Masorti Judaism, tikkun olam and leadership education.

name: Maccabi Gap Year
location: Tel Aviv, Jerusalem, and kibbutz placements
goodFor: For post-high-schoolers wanting a fully supervised Maccabi-movement gap year mixing leadership seminars, a taste of IDF Marva and MDA training, kibbutz work, and touring, over a flexible 5-10 month length.

name: Kol Ami Mechina
organization: Kol Ami - The Jewish Peoplehood Leadership Academy
durationText: 6 months (end of August-end of February), with an optional 3+ month extension
goodFor: For young Israelis and diaspora peers who want to spend 6 months together in a Jewish Peoplehood leadership program, with an optional 3+ month internship extension.

name: Israel Government Fellows (IGF)
organization: Menachem Begin Heritage Center, in cooperation with the Office of the Prime Minister of Israel and Masa Israel Journey
goodFor: For young adults wanting an elite, hands-on 10-month government/policy internship inside Israeli ministries and NGOs, paired with intensive Hebrew study and weekly access to top Israeli officials and policymakers.

name: Hasbara Fellowships Israel Training Program
goodFor: For North American college-student leaders wanting an intensive 16-day Israel advocacy and leadership crash course, meeting Israeli political, military and business figures and both Israelis and Palestinians firsthand.

name: ELI Gap Year Program
durationText: Full academic year (August-May) or a single semester
cost: $14,000/semester ($28,000/year); includes housing, insurance, tours and full academic credit, excludes flights/food/personal expenses
goodFor: For recent high school graduates (3.0+ GPA) wanting a brand-new (2026-27 launch) Tel Aviv University gap year pairing for-credit coursework with cybersecurity/AI/entrepreneurship workshops and mentorship from Israeli founders.
note: TAU describes it as 'Jewish-focused but open to Jewish-adjacent communities' — tagged pluralistic as the closer fit to secular; worth a human read of TAU's own framing.

name: Technion American Medical School (TeAMS)
organization: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology, Ruth and Bruce Rappaport Faculty of Medicine
goodFor: For US/Canadian pre-med graduates (MCAT required) wanting an English-language M.D. degree in Haifa preparing them specifically to practice back in North America.
note: Track is explicitly built for North America-bound students; unclear from public sources how much coursework is shared with Israeli MD students day-to-day.

name: Jerusalem College of Technology - International English Speakers Program
durationText: 1-year program, or full multi-year degree track
goodFor: For English-speaking men wanting to combine a real yeshiva learning schedule (mornings) with an accredited Computer Science or Business Administration degree taught in English (afternoons).
note: Researched specifically JCT's men's program page; JCT may run a parallel women's track (Machon Tal) not covered here — worth a follow-up batch if so.

name: Ono Academic College International School
durationText: Full degree program (multi-year); semester options vary by track
goodFor: For international students, Olim, and globally minded applicants wanting an English/Hebrew-hybrid Israeli degree — the International Law LLB is the flagship track — while gradually integrating into a large, multicultural campus.

name: Shenkar College International Program
organization: Shenkar College of Engineering, Design and Art
goodFor: For international students 18+ wanting one semester of English-taught art, design or engineering coursework at a school that fuses all three disciplines, with hands-on workshops taught in simplified Hebrew.

name: Emunah College - Faculty of Arts and Design
goodFor: For religious women wanting an accredited multi-year arts, design or drama degree, with dedicated English-language coursework support built in.

name: Buchmann-Mehta School of Music International Program
organization: Tel Aviv University, in partnership with the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra
durationText: Bachelor's (4 years), Master's (2 years), or Artist Diploma
cost: Full-tuition scholarship for all accepted international students; a limited number also receive a living stipend and/or housing scholarship
goodFor: For outstanding international musicians (audition required) wanting a fully-funded Bachelor's, Master's, or Artist Diploma studying under Israel Philharmonic principal players in Tel Aviv.

name: Israel Lacrosse Gap Year Program
goodFor: For lacrosse players (18-30) wanting a gap year built around competing and coaching in Israel — training, league games, European tournaments, and mentoring local youth teams as sport ambassadors.

name: EYAHT - Aish HaTorah College for Women
goodFor: For unaffiliated, college-educated professional women (22-30) wanting a flexible-length, no-background-assumed introduction to Torah Judaism designed specifically for women exploring observance.
